tweets.text:
Friendly reminder that the MOU (between US and Iran) has been the front and center news item for the last 2 weeks. 

We went from, within hours, to within days, to now: “Up to Trump and Iran’s Supreme Leader.”

Meanwhile, traders won’t buy anything ahead of the MOU, so we are just dragging ass. Market has delayed demand destruction pricing and we are drawing onshore oil inventories at the fastest rate in history.

tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
A New Glenn rocket has 5.5 GWh of energy (first and second stage).

The Little Boy bomb dropped on Hiroshima had 17.5 GWh of energy.

The largest non-nuclear blast was Operation Minor Scale (1985) with 4.65 GWh of energy.

The largest non-nuclear accidental blast was the Halifax

tweets.text:
fwiw, 3km/s is very weak for a detonation in the scales of chemical explosives

and if we're talking about reality, obviously the fuel/o2 arent well mixed at all, so this probably going to be closer to a 500 m/s, just enough for a beautiful shock wave
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
the detonation velocity of a "perfectly" mixed oxygen hydrogen / methane mixture is only around 3000m/s, which is why the shock wave (clearly visible and large) is almost surprising

maybe the  initial explosion destroyed a stage completely, leading to full on detonation https://t.co/a5OBkQlItp
